hospitalizations.principal_dx_description: Presence of right artificial hip joint
hospitalizations.reason: Subcutaneous infusion port placement (interventional radiology procedure) prior to initiation of FOLFOX/bevacizumab chemotherapy
Grade 3 neutropenia (ANC 0.9 K/uL) with low-grade fever following cycle 8 FOLFOX/bevacizumab; managed with IV fluids, G-CSF support, and monitoring; afebrile at discharge
Hypertensive urgency (BP 174/108 mmHg) attributed to ramucirumab during cycle 5 of FOLFIRI/ramucirumab; antihypertensive regimen intensified; discharged with blood pressure controlled
Grade 3 neutropenia (ANC 0.7 K/uL) during cycle 2 of TAS-102/bevacizumab, day 15 check; managed with filgrastim; TAS-102 dose held and subsequently reduced to 30 mg/m2
